prompt: The deploy-service team escalated on us twice this week and both bugs are in our inventory client. First: their validator started rejecting some of our sync payloads with 400 `tags: expected array, got null` — their contract says tags is ALWAYS a JSON array, [] when a component has none, and we clearly emit null sometimes (a record that came in over the wire with "tags":null re-syncs as null too, so it keeps ping-ponging). Second, nastier one: an on-call script sorted the Tags slice it got back from our cache and suddenly every later reader saw the reordered tags — you can reproduce with plain writes too: Sync a record, Get it, assign to tags[0], Get it again and the cached record is corrupted. Records handed out of the cache are supposed to be snapshots. Please fix inventory.go so encoding always meets the wire contract (empty array never null, notes still omitted when empty, decode→encode round trips stable) and so nothing returned from Sync or Get shares state with what the cache holds. Don't touch inventory_test.go. Verify with go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. repro_cmd on the baseline: TestEncodeEmitsEmptyTagArrayNotNull and TestDecodedNullTagsReencodeAsArray fail because nil Tags marshal as null; TestGetHandsOutIndependentCopies and TestSyncResultIsIndependentOfCache fail because the returned Component shares its Tags backing array with the cached record, so caller writes corrupt the cache.
